title: NOC 80010 – Managers in natural resources production and fishing | GTR Immigration
description: Managers under NOC 80010 are the strategic leaders overseeing Canada's primary resource sectors, including forestry, logging, mining, quarrying, and commercial fishing. As a TEER 0 (Management) occupation, these professionals are responsible for production quotas, safety compliance, and environmental stewardship in some of the most economically significant industries in the country. In 2026, as Canada balances resource extraction with net-zero environmental targets, these managers are in high demand to lead technological transitions. For immigration, this high-level role is a premier candidate for Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker) and specialized Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category8 – Natural resources, agriculture and related production occupationsTEER Level0 (Management)Major Group80 – Management occupations in natural resources, agriculture and related productionSub-Major Group800 – Management occupations in natural resources, agriculture and related productionMinor Group8001 – Managers in natural resources production and fishingNOC Code80010

duties: Plan, organize, direct, and control the operations of establishments in forestry, logging, mining, quarrying, and commercial fishing.
Develop and implement production schedules and operational policies to meet corporate and environmental targets.
Evaluate and report on production efficiency and oversee the maintenance of heavy machinery and equipment.
Ensure strict compliance with occupational health and safety regulations and environmental protection laws.
Manage budgets, negotiate contracts with suppliers or buyers, and oversee the hiring and training of technical staff.
Coordinate with government agencies and indigenous communities regarding land use and resource rights.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree in a relevant field (e.g., Mining Engineering, Forestry, Earth Sciences) is typically required.
Several years of experience in the specific industry (e.g., as a supervisor or technical specialist) are mandatory.
Professional Certification: Eligibility for provincial professional registration (e.g., Registered Professional Forester or Professional Engineer) is often required.
Strong leadership, financial management, and strategic planning skills are essential.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the transition to "Green Mining" and "Precision Forestry" is creating a need for a new generation of managers who can integrate technology with traditional resource management. Retirements of senior personnel in the mining and forestry sectors will also create significant openings through 2028.
labour_market_demand: The demand for managers in natural resources is Good to Very Good, particularly in resource-rich provinces. In 2026, British Columbia and Quebec are seeing high demand in forestry management, while Ontario, Saskatchewan, and Newfoundland and Labrador are prioritizing managers in the mining and sub-sea resource sectors. As global demand for critical minerals grows, the requirement for experienced production managers has become a national priority.
Global Industry Leadership: Lead operations in a country that is a world leader in sustainable mining and forestry practices.
High Compensation: As a TEER 0 management role, this occupation offers some of the highest salary brackets in the Canadian labor market.
Technological Innovation: Oversee the implementation of AI-driven resource mapping, automated harvesting, and green extraction technologies.
Direct Path to PR: Management experience is highly weighted in the Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) for Express Entry.
pathways: Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker): High CRS points due to the management level (TEER 0).
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Targeted draws for the natural resources sector.
Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): Corporate and Human Capital priorities.
Saskatchewan INP (Occupations In-Demand): High priority for mining and agriculture-adjacent management.
